messaging_angles: Crypto debit card with cashback rewards
messaging_analysis.buy_crypto_framing: direct — explicitly pushes trading and buying crypto with clear CTAs like 'start trading', 'set recurring buys', and trade-to-win mechanics that require actual transactions
messaging_analysis.fees_messaging: Completely avoided — no mention of fees, costs, or pricing across any ads
messaging_analysis.simplicity_messaging: Moderately emphasized — positioning as 'simple', 'easy', 'in seconds/minutes', with beginner tutorials mentioned, but not the dominant theme. Focuses on accessibility for both beginners and pros ('Trade your way')
messaging_analysis.security_messaging: Lightly emphasized — mentioned explicitly in only 2 ads via 'Perfect security score on Kaiko' credential and Krak Card security feature. Not a leading message but used as a trust signal
messaging_analysis.dominant_format: carousel — 14 of 15 ads are carousel format, only 1 image ad
longest_running_themes: Massive crypto selection (520+ cryptocurrencies) — seen in 144d and 58d running ads
Recurring/automated crypto buys for long-term strategy — 160d running ad
Crypto debit card (Krak Card) — 70-71d running ads
messaging_analysis.summary: Kraken's ad strategy balances promotional hooks (Bitcoin giveaway contests requiring trading activity) with evergreen product benefits (massive coin selection, automated investing, crypto card). The longest-running ads focus on passive income strategies (staking, recurring buys, cashback card) and breadth of offering rather than contests, suggesting these educational/utility angles have stronger performance than short-term promotional campaigns.
